title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Patriots Charitable Foundation.
Despite a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh returned to running through physical therapy and support.
McHugh honors her late mentor, Susan Hurley, by running, emphasizing cancer won't have the final word in her life.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and Oregon senators demand Meta clarify its plans for facial recognition in smart glasses.
Markey warns the technology could threaten privacy and discourage public protest by identifying protesters in real time.
Meta has until April 6 to explain data handling and opt-out options for potential facial recognition use.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS.
The proposed funding is for PFAS-free turnout gear after current equipment tested positive for harmful chemicals.
Firefighters expressed disappointment over the delay, citing health risks associated with PFAS exposure.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes turning Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address rising costs and $57 million in repairs.
Zoo to remain open until September, then allow limited free weekend access as it transitions to wildlife rehabilitation and conservation focus.
A transition team will form in July to oversee changes; critics, including zoo veterinarian, question the plan's feasibility.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown had a last-minute microwedding in Somerville after blizzard cancellations on January 26, 2026.
The FX series "Love Story" explores John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's romance and features 1990s communication nostalgia.
Meredith Goldstein questions the use of 'partner' for new relationships in her Love Letters column about modern relationship terms.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback from Burlington is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late father and his own cancer survival.
Averback began running after his father's death and his own cancer diagnosis, finding it therapeutic and a form of progress.
He is participating with Team MGH, linking his run to Massachusetts General Hospital's role in his family's health journey.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard and Eagles WR A.J. Brown are working out together, fueling trade rumors.
The Patriots reportedly made an offer for Brown, but the Eagles rejected it waiting until June to trade.
Philadelphia seeks at least a first-round draft pick for Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler with 1,000-yard seasons.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ended his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins are considering signing Hagens to an entry-level contract after BC's season ended.
GM Don Sweeney emphasized flexibility in incorporating prospects like Hagens into the Bruins' lineup.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' features Bradley Cooper and Will Arnett in a comedy-drama, now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' continues the series, with Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan, streaming on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, explores professional bowling, available on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora's contract with the Red Sox extends through the 2027 season, with discussions of an extension potentially in 2026.
Cora is in his eighth season managing the Red Sox, aiming to surpass Terry Francona in managerial wins.
Family considerations, including his sons' education, will influence Cora's future decisions post-2027.
